message:
🐛 fix: self-upgrade broadcasts progress:20 only after patch succeeds (#7976) (#8041)

The TriggerUpgrade handler used to emit a `progress: 20` WebSocket
message ("Patching deployment image to X") BEFORE calling
Deployments.Patch. If the patch then failed (RBAC, API server error,
network issue), clients saw "20% — Patching..." immediately followed by
a "failed" terminal event, giving the misleading impression that
something had already been mutated when nothing had.

Move the `progress: 20` broadcast to AFTER the successful Patch call
and reword it in the past tense ("Deployment image patched to X"). On
the failure path, no intermediate progress is claimed at all — the UI
transitions directly from idle to failed.

Fixes #7976

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
